title: ‘The Beatles: Get Back’ and Our Fab 5 Beatles Albums
format: Sean and Amanda spend Thanksgiving weekend with Peter Jackson's eight-hour Beatles documentary, then rank their favorite Beatles albums. Later, Sean talks to Penny Lane about her Kenny G documentary for the Music Box series.

segments.heading: 'Get Back' and the ghost of 'Let It Be'

films.title: Let It Be
films.note: Michael Lindsay-Hogg's original 80-minute film, drawn from the same footage but rarely seen since. Jackson's cut restores what it left out, including Paul writing the title track in real time.

films.title: The Magic Christian
films.note: Mentioned in passing: the whole 'Get Back' session was bookended by Ringo's commitment to go shoot this Peter Sellers comedy.

segments.heading: In conversation
segments.note: the latest in the Music Box series, premiering on HBO

films.title: Listening to Kenny G
films.note: Penny Lane's documentary uses the smooth-jazz star to work through a question from her years teaching art students: whether there's a meaningful line between good art and bad art.

films.title: Hail Satan?
films.note: Lane's previous film, referenced as a companion piece. Sean notes both share her knack for making serious ideas go down easy.

films.title: The Velvet Underground
films.note: Lane's answer to the show's closing question: the last great thing she saw, and an inspiration for how a music documentary can foreground the experience of the music itself.

films.title: Confessions of a Good Samaritan
films.note: Lane's next project, then in post-production, about her own experience donating a kidney to a stranger.
